skill.name: seo-cannibalization-detector
skill.description: Analyzes provided pages to find keyword overlap, search intent conflicts, and SEO cannibalization risks. Recommends differentiation, consolidation, canonical, and internal linking fixes.
skill.summary: Finds SEO cannibalization between similar pages and recommends practical resolution strategies.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and common SEO wording as weak cryptography. Manual review found no executable code, no network access, no data exfiltration, and no prompt injection attempt in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 25 and 78-89 use Markdown formatting for a resource filename and an output template, not Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are inside Markdown prose and a fenced example block. No command, interpreter, variable interpolation, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Lines 3, 44, 62, and 118 contain SEO words such as description or descriptions, not DES, MD5, SHA1, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are plain SEO guidance about page metadata. There is no cryptographic operation, dependency, key material, or hashing behavior.

content.value_statement: Similar pages can compete for the same keywords and weaken organic performance. This skill compares provided pages and recommends clear fixes.
actual_capabilities: Compares target keywords across multiple provided pages.
Identifies overlapping search intent and similar topic coverage.
Flags title, meta description, heading, and internal anchor conflicts.
Produces a cannibalization report with competing page inventory.
Recommends consolidation, differentiation, canonical, redirect, and linking tactics.
Suggests prevention workflows for content planning and recurring audits.
limitations: Requires page URLs, page content, rankings, or keyword data from the user.
Does not crawl websites or fetch live search ranking data by itself.
Cannot verify implementation in a CMS without additional tool access.
Recommendations need review against business goals and analytics data.

use_cases.title: Audit competing blog posts
use_cases.description: Compare similar articles and decide whether to merge, differentiate, or retarget them.

use_cases.title: Plan keyword assignments
use_cases.description: Map primary keywords to unique pages before publishing a new content cluster.

use_cases.title: Prioritize technical fixes
use_cases.description: Turn overlapping metadata, canonicals, redirects, and internal links into a ranked cleanup plan.

prompt_templates.title: Basic page comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of two similar pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these two pages for SEO cannibalization. Identify shared keywords, search intent overlap, and the best next action.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword overlap matrix
prompt_templates.scenario: Review several pages in one topic cluster
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these pages and create a keyword overlap matrix. Show which pages compete and which keywords need clearer ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Resolution strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose fixes for known competing pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate these competing pages with rankings and conversion goals. Recommend whether to consolidate, differentiate, canonicalize, redirect, or adjust internal links.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced prevention workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a recurring cannibalization control process
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a cannibalization prevention workflow for this content team. Include keyword mapping rules, pre-publish checks, monitoring cadence, and escalation criteria.

output_examples.input: Two product guide pages both target best project management software and cover the same buyer intent.
output_examples.output: The report identifies a high overlap conflict, recommends one authoritative guide, and suggests redirecting or retargeting the weaker page.

output_examples.input: A blog cluster includes five articles about technical SEO audits with similar headings and anchors.
output: A keyword overlap matrix groups the competing pages by intent.
The resolution list assigns one primary keyword to each page.
The internal link plan updates repeated anchor text.

output_examples.input: A category page and an informational article both rank for the same commercial keyword.
output_examples.output: The analysis separates transactional and informational intent, then recommends metadata changes and clearer internal linking signals.

best_practices: Provide URLs, page titles, meta descriptions, headings, target keywords, and ranking data when available.
Review recommendations against conversion goals before merging or redirecting pages.
Run the check before publishing new pages in an existing topic cluster.
anti_patterns: Do not expect live crawling or ranking checks without supplying external data.
Do not consolidate pages only because they share one keyword.
Do not apply canonicals or redirects without checking user intent and business value.

faq.question: What is SEO cannibalization?
faq.answer: It happens when multiple pages compete for the same keyword or search intent and reduce each page's clarity.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide page URLs, titles, meta descriptions, headings, body summaries, target keywords, rankings, and traffic data when possible.

faq.question: Can this skill crawl my site?
faq.answer: No. It analyzes information you provide and does not include a crawler or live search data connection.

faq.question: Does it always recommend merging pages?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend differentiation, canonical tags, redirects, internal link updates, or no action.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: How should I validate recommendations?
faq.answer: Check rankings, traffic, conversions, backlinks, and stakeholder goals before changing live pages.
